content_of_post_to_comment: Feel like i've been struggling with being Disciplined recently and keeping motivated, I spend more time just doom scrolling and just being sat rather than being active like I normally am, snoozing the alarm and not getting out of bed when it goes off. Little things like not having the motivation and not having the discipline to even brush teeth in the morning even though I have a irrational fear of the dentist, or cooking good meals, getting outside, I dont think the heat helps and having a calf problem has stopped my running. I am going to work abroud soon in a remote area where there will be no phone signal, long early wake ups and a general shift which I hope will help but has anyone got any tips for keepinggoing and not slipping away. Had a simula rproblem when I was finishing my uni exams and even though I did well I couldn't get going for reading articles and making good notes. Any advice would be much appreciated, sorry for the life long intro!
comment: The phone scrolling thing is a sneaky one because it makes starting anything feel harder than it actually is. I've found it helps to lower the bar ridiculously low for a while, like just getting outside for 5 minutes or brushing teeth before doing anything else. Waiting to feel motivated usually doesn't work; a tiny bit of action tends to bring motivation back.
